name: Embalse de Benínar — circular from Berja
name_original: Embalse de Benínar — circular desde Berja
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Campo Dalias offer diverse landscapes in Almería, Spain. The region features coastal areas with beaches and nature reserves, alongside the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Gádor. An extensive agricultural landscape, characterized by greenhouses, also defines parts of the area. Historical sites such as castles and towers are often found along coastal paths. Best touring cycling routes around Campo Dalias The most popular touring cycling route is Monumento Natural Arrecife Barrera de Posidonia — circular from Roquetas de Mar, a 16.6 miles (26.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This easy route explores a coastal nature reserve. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Paseo de Poniente de Almerimar — circular from El Ejido, a moderate 20.3 miles (32.7 km) path. This route offers coastal views along the promenade. Local touring cyclists also love the Embalse de Benínar — circular from Berja, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Touring cycling around Campo Dalias is defined by coastal paths, mountainous roads, and an agricultural landscape. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging climbs. The routes in Campo Dalias are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 6,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Campo Dalias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Campo Dalias?
A: There are over 240 touring cycling routes in Campo Dalias, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes routes along the coast, through agricultural landscapes, and into the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Gádor.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see on a touring cycling route in Campo Dalias?
A: Touring cyclists in Campo Dalias can experience diverse landscapes. Routes often feature scenic coastal views, such as those around the Golfo de Almería, with opportunities to see beaches like Playa del Faro and Playa de Roquetas de Mar. You'll also find paths through the unique agricultural landscape characterized by extensive greenhouses, and more challenging rides in the mountainous areas near the Sierra de Gádor.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Campo Dalias offers many easy touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1054922773/punta-entinas-sabinar-nature-reserve-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 18.3-mile (29.4 km) route that takes you through a coastal nature reserve. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40329455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle – Playa de Roquetas de Mar loop from Plaza de Vasco de Gama&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.7 miles (23.6 km) and also considered easy.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Campo Dalias?
A: Campo Dalias benefits from a dry, mild Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for outdoor activities for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying comfortable conditions.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Campo Dalias?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Campo Dalias are circular. Popular options include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041894128/monumento-natural-arrecife-barrera-de-posidonia-circular-from-roquetas-de-mar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumento Natural Arrecife Barrera de Posidonia — circular from Roquetas de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, which explores a coastal nature reserve, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1054896377/paseo-de-poniente-de-almerimar-circular-from-el-ejido" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de Poniente de Almerimar — circular from El Ejido&lt;/a&gt;, offering coastal views along the promenade.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the coastal cycling paths, you can encounter well-preserved historical sites. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/744872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardias Viejas Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31888057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the Torre de Cerrillos (Cerrillos Tower). Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31537849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almerimar Tower – Guardias Viejas Castle loop from Balerma&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight these historical points.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while cycling in Campo Dalias?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities for wildlife spotting, especially in its natural areas. The nature reserve Punta Sabina, for instance, is known for wild flamingos. Routes that pass through natural parks or wetlands, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1054922773/punta-entinas-sabinar-nature-reserve-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve Loop&lt;/a&gt;, are good places to look for local fauna.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Campo Dalias offers routes with significant elevation changes, particularly in the areas closer to the Sierra de Gádor mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046208759/embalse-de-beninar-circular-from-berja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Benínar — circular from Berja&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.4-mile (26.4 km) trail that leads through mountainous terrain, featuring substantial climbs.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Campo Dalias?
A: The touring cycling routes in Campo Dalias are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 900 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from coastal paths to mountainous roads, and the well-maintained network that caters to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or points of interest along the routes?
A: Beyond historical sites, you can explore several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1383204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas and Wetlands of Roquetas de Mar&lt;/a&gt; are a significant natural monument, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4103980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de la Chanata&lt;/a&gt; offers a serene lake environment. Many routes also provide access to beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1401503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for a touring cycling route in Campo Dalias?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Campo Dalias varies depending on the distance and difficulty. Many popular routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041894128/monumento-natural-arrecife-barrera-de-posidonia-circular-from-roquetas-de-mar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumento Natural Arrecife Barrera de Posidonia — circular from Roquetas de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes to 2 hours. Longer or more challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14603355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Roquetas de Mar – Santa Ana Castle loop from Aguadulce&lt;/a&gt;, might take over 3 hours.
tips: Located in the heart of the Poniente Almeria region, Berja is a municipality full of history and culture. Its historic center is a true treasure with churches, hermitages, palaces and charming buildings. In addition, it has an important natural wealth, with the Sierra Nevada Natural Park as a backdrop.

Reservoir fed by the waters of the Grande de Adra river that descends from Sierra Nevada. The name of the reservoir is due to the fact that it was built on the town of Beninar, which has been left under its waters. The area is surrounded by mountains and rich vegetation, offers a spectacular view of nature.

waypoints.name: Church of the Annunciation of Berja
